Good question, this is legacy code, and I think the label came from copying and pasting from the test above.
Will modify the label now, as it only returns on cm handle.
But I know that these  were put in here to test the multiple cm handles which the watchdog would detect and what it would do with each cm handle until it detected no new cm handles to be worked on(as represented by null here).

i.e when state moves from ADVISED to READY  OR ADVISED to LOCKED..

Good question. I can't remember if we had a discussion around this. I feel it's best to state to the end user that the state is already set.
What does the team think?

There is a similar one, but that is to set the composite state to READY, along with setting the initial sync state. This is being used after that process. I can look into creating a similar method within compositeStateUtils for this process.
Log.debug is used above too. Which is why I used it here for consistency.
Cool. As we all discussed , we will keep throwing the exception and in parallel we can check with stakeholders on this.

After looking into this, if we set the state to true after already going through the data sync process it will reset the data sync state. So I feel this is the best approach to combat this

Have took Tony comments on board from the meeting, and will silently ignore. Open to change based on teams preference

This is done so to resolve the edgecase if a state is set to false during the sync process.

Outlined in open questions number 4 here 

https://jira.onap.org/browse/CPS-1133
This is needed, or else the test will continue loop while the unsynchronized cm handle isn't equal to null, because the process will continue to look for cm handles until no more are found.

Just commenting here so doesn't get lost in previous patch, I just meant the equals true bit isnt needed since unSynchronizedReadyCmHandle.getCompositeState().getDataSyncEnabled() returns a boolean anyway, otherwise its true.equals(true) returns true if ye get me, not big deal either way :)
Ah grand sorry makes sense thanks!
Sonar, will complain if a primitive boolean expression isn't being used.

I specified flag, because we also have the data sync watchdog, and I wanted to avoid any confusion with this.

So the requirement is :

1. If we are trying to set the flag as it is before then ignore it.
2. we are not allowed to set the flag of a cmHandle if it is not in READY state.

Correct?
here it takes in other values as well.

Exactly, in line with the A/C of the story, we will do both of these.
The silently ignoring the data sync enabled was brought up in the CPS meeting last Friday, instead of throwing a data validation exception.
